title: NR Cell Measurement Events
description: Generic 5G NR measurement event configuration used by UE RRC measurement reporting. These are normal cell-measurement triggers; A3/A5 are the ones most directly related to handover.
source: 3GPP TS 38.331 V17.10.0: ReportConfigNR / EventTriggerConfig lists eventA1 through eventA6; inter-RAT reporting lists eventB1 and eventB2.

logEvidence.summary: In logs, inspect 0xB821 NR5G RRC OTA Packet downlink RRCReconfiguration. Measurement events are normally configured under measConfig -> reportConfigToAddModList -> reportType -> eventTriggered -> eventId.

sampleObservation: For 5g_0523_01.txt, the observed NR measurement event configs are A1, A2, A3, A4, and A5. A6 is not observed. B1/B2 are also not observed as the trigger for this NR handover.

events.condition: Serving cell becomes better than a threshold.
events.handoverRelation: Often used to stop or relax measurement/reporting when the serving cell is good again.

events.condition: Serving cell becomes worse than a threshold.
events.handoverRelation: Often used to start inter-frequency or inter-RAT measurement when serving quality degrades.

events.condition: Neighbor cell becomes better than the serving cell by an offset.
events.handoverRelation: Most common handover trigger: the neighbor becomes sufficiently better than the current serving cell.

events.condition: Neighbor cell becomes better than a threshold.
events.handoverRelation: Neighbor is good enough in absolute terms; can support mobility decisions.

events.condition: Serving cell is worse than threshold1 and neighbor cell is better than threshold2.
events.handoverRelation: Common handover condition with stricter two-sided criteria.

events.condition: Neighbor cell becomes better than an SCell by an offset.
events.handoverRelation: Mainly SCell / carrier-aggregation related; not the most common ordinary PCell handover event.

interRatEvents.condition: Inter-RAT neighbor becomes better than a threshold.
interRatEvents.handoverRelation: Used for NR to LTE/UTRA or other inter-RAT mobility measurement.

interRatEvents.condition: Serving NR cell is worse than threshold1 and inter-RAT neighbor is better than threshold2.
interRatEvents.handoverRelation: Inter-RAT mobility trigger with both serving degradation and target quality conditions.
